results.title: Tyson Foods AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Tyson Foods has positioned itself as a leader in integrating artificial intelligence across its food production and distribution operations[1]. The company has invested $1.3 billion in automation initiatives over three years, with AI and predictive analytics expected to deliver $1 billion in recurring productivity savings by fiscal 2024[2]. Tyson's AI strategy encompasses computer vision for quality control and inventory management, AWS-powered solutions for real-time production insights, and an AI-driven conversational assistant for customer engagement[3]. Through its venture capital arm Tyson Ventures, the company has invested over $100 million since 2016 in emerging technologies and selected six AI startups at its 2025 Demo Day, focusing on innovations like antimicrobial proteins and virtual R&D scientists[4].

Generative AI assistant for Tyson Foodservice website to enhance customer search and engagement
...
Built using Amazon Bedrock with Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet, replacing keyword-based search with semantic search. Enables engagement with over 1 million unattended operators who purchase through distributors
...
Tyson uses AI for computer vision systems that automatically count chicken trays, detect equipment anomalies, and optimize production processes. They also deploy predictive analytics for supply chain optimization and real-time production insights.
...
Tyson has strategic partnerships with AWS for cloud-based AI services, the AWS Generative AI Innovation Center for conversational AI development, and Guild Education for AI-enhanced workforce training programs.
...
Through Tyson Ventures, they selected six AI startups in 2025 including Prevera (antimicrobial proteins), Proxy Foods (AI virtual scientist), and Orby AI (enterprise automation), among others focused on food innovation.
...
Tyson deployed an AI-powered conversational assistant on their foodservice website that uses semantic search to help customers find products more easily and provides personalized recommendations and support for over 1 million food operators.

- Hybrid-Movie-Recommendation-System - 1 stars • 4 contributions
  We make use of python Pandas Library to analyze the data and prepare a structured data. We then ingest the structured data built using python into graph database (neo4j). Graph databases are very effective in establishing the networks and studying the relationship between the users.

- Smart-Collision-Detection - 2 contributions
  This project demonstrates the use of Smart Phone Sensors and Machine Learning to detect the collision. The proposed system can be divided into two modules. Module 1 includes developing a mobile application for collecting data from the on-board sensors. Module 2 includes processing and classification of the data collected. We leverage a Machine Learning model in tandem with a neural network for classification of collision sounds collected from the smart phone.

results.title: Melody Gao
highlights:
Data Scientist at [Tyson Foods](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tyson-foods)
...
I’m a data scientist and machine learning engineer who loves turning complex data problems into practical, high-impact solutions. My work lives at the intersection of analytics, strategy, and execution — helping organizations make smarter and more confident decisions. At Tyson Foods, I lead the Media Allocation & Performance (MAP) Optimizer, a large-scale initiative that transforms how marketing investments are planned and measured across brands like Jimmy Dean, Hillshire Farm, and Ball Park. Partnering closely with marketing, engineering, and analytics teams, I build models and tools that connect performance data with business strategy, improving transparency, ROI, and decision speed. Before Tyson, I led and co-developed data-driven projects across industries: • University of Chicago – Built a real-time conversation system to capture and summarize doctor-patient interactions, improving documentation accuracy and workflow efficiency. • Deloitte – Designed a two-stage modeling framework to measure marketing lift and forecast campaign impact, increasing ROI precision and revenue predictability. • Rogers Communications – Developed an end-to-end data pipeline that turned network telemetry into actionable insights for reliability and customer experience improvements. Across every role, I focus on bridging technical depth with business context — creating systems that not only analyze data but also drive measurable outcomes. I’m passionate about transforming how organizations use data to plan, adapt, and grow.
...
### Data Scientist at [Tyson Foods](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tyson-foods) (Current)
...
I lead the Media Allocation & Performance (MAP) Optimizer, a cross-functional initiative that transforms how Tyson allocates and evaluates marketing spend across major brands, including Jimmy Dean, Hillshire Farm, and Ball Park. The project combines data engineering, marketing analytics, and optimization modelling to help brand and marketing teams make faster, more transparent investment decisions.
...
- Designed and implemented the MAP Optimizer blueprint, integrating marketing-mix results, retailer closed-loop data, and incremental ROI metrics into a unified decision framework.
...
- Partnered with marketing analysts and data engineers to automate data ingestion and model refreshes within Tyson’s Compass environment, improving speed and consistency of performance reporting.
...
- Built forecasting and optimization models that guide weekly media allocation at the brand × tactic × platform × location level, driving measurable ROI and margin improvement.
...
- Collaborated across analytics, marketing, and technology teams to ensure outputs are business-ready, explainable, and scalable.
...
- Contributed to the design of internal experimentation and uplift-measurement methods to validate optimizer recommendations and track incremental impact.
...
This project builds a complete causal machine learning system designed to precisely evaluate the true impact (uplift) of advertising campaigns and develop optimal allocation strategies to maximize ROI (Return on Investment). It covers the full industrial workflow—including data preprocessing, uplift model development, multi-model evaluation, and policy simulation—and is applicable to precision marketing scenarios in industries such as e-commerce, finance, and SaaS. Project Objectives • Estimate the individualized causal effect of ads (CATE) using real or quasi-experimental data • Identify high-value user groups who convert only because of the advertisement • Optimize budget allocation based on uplift score ranking • Improve overall ROI by avoiding wasted spend on users who would convert naturally or cannot be influenced Show less

results.title: Ameya Jaykumar Jadhav
highlights:
Lead Data Engineer at Tyson Foods | Data Engineer | Data Analyst | Software Development Engineer | Data Scientist | Business Analyst
...
Lead Data Engineer at [Tyson Foods](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tyson-foods)
...
### Lead Data Engineer at [Tyson Foods](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tyson-foods) (Current)
...
Defined, designed, implemented, and managed cloud ETL data pipelines with high data quality standards. Developed automated, monitored, and data-driven cloud solutions, translating strategic goals into practical success for a team. Prioritized testability and maintainability, ensuring our data infrastructure stays reliable and data quality is maintained. Designed, developed, and rigorously tested custom applications and data flows, supporting projects, releases, and the production environment. Worked closely with cross-functional teams to make projects run smoothly and produce high-quality results. Used JIRA to keep our work top-notch and tracked tasks for better collaboration. Kept an eye out for issues and shared them with the team, encouraging everyone to watch out for each other. Worked on gathering and understanding the business requirements and data to design/support various reports and data warehousing. Used CI/CD tools like GitLab to work together seamlessly. Enabled team growth through automated processes, custom tools, and cutting-edge technologies.
...
Managed team of 10 people and point of contact for technical troubleshooting, maintenance and application development for library services. Prepared reports, Dashboards, visualizations using Tableau for staff and Deans office for monthly statistics data. Conducted weekly Technical workshops for Library staff, students and faculty. Resolved issues with large data tables in Excel and Google Docs. Automated generation of library stats (SUMA, Zeta & KIC) using python, saving 5+ hours of manual work weekly. Developed a python script using ML to forecast ink refill in printers from paper cut data. Developed multiple Single Page Application (SPA). Developed a virtual assistant skill for Amazon Alexa using NodeJS and AWS Lambda function, which reduced the workload to 50%. Created AWS Code Pipeline, a service that builds, tests, and deploys code every time there is a code change. Developed an application that serves the library management for taking the 3D print jobs online using Java APIs as middleware to interact UI with MongoDB database. Performed bulk load of JSON data from AWS s3 bucket to Snowflake. Used Snowflake functions to perform semi structures data parsing entirely with SQL statements. Used Amazon DynamoDB to establish database connection with Alexa skill. Implemented GIT version controlling for the current projects. Developed Single-Page Applications for GIS-based DMAT to visualize millions of data on the map utilizing ArcGIS, Java, REST Services, and, PostgreSQL. Created Cloud BigTable to switch from a traditional relational database and initiated machine learning applications for the future.

highlights:

### Instructional Designer
...
###### Tyson Foods
...
Employee | Full-Time | $63K - $107K
...
On-Site | Springdale, AR
...
June 23, 2026
...
Job Details:
...
Job Purpose/Summary:
...
The Instructional Designer will be responsible for designing and developing engaging and effective learning materials for our organization. You will collaborate with subject matter experts, trainers, and other stakeholders to create instructional content that meets the needs of our learners. This position also helps administer the company’s Learning Management System.
...
Essential Duties and Responsibilities:
...
- Collaborate with subject matter experts to analyze learning needs and develop learning objectives
...
- Design and develop instructional materials, including e-learning courses, instructor-led training materials, job aids, and assessments
...
- Apply instructional design principles and adult learning theory to create engaging and interactive learning experiences
...
- Utilize industry-standard tools such as Articulate 360 (Rise & Storyline), Workday Learning, Camtasia, and Synthesia to develop e-learning modules and multimedia content
...
- Incorporate multimedia elements, such as graphics, audio, and video, to enhance learning materials
...
- Conduct quality assurance checks to ensure accuracy, consistency, and adherence to instructional design standards
...
- Revise and update existing learning materials to ensure relevance and effectiveness
...
- Stay current with industry trends and best practices in instructional design and e-learning development
...
- Support ad hoc projects and assignments, as needed
...
Experience: At least 1 year of instructional design experience
...
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Instructional Design, HR, Education, Business or a related field
...
Computer Skills: Standard computer skills. Familiarity with learning management systems (LMS, ideally Workday) and learning technologies
...
Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills
...
Special Skills: Ability to build curriculum through storytelling by incorporating case studies, real-life examples or fictional scenarios. Strong organizational skills. Attention to detail and commitment to producing high-quality work. Experience creating targeted content that generates measurable ROI
...

Attending #SAP Sapphire 2026 for the first time and representing Gainwell Technologies was both an honor and an incredible learning experience.
...
Over the past few days,
...
had the opportunity to engage with SAP leaders, industry experts, and transformation leaders who are actively shaping the future of enterprise technology.
...
✅ AI is no longer an innovation initiative. It is becoming the operating model. SAP’s vision of an AI-first Autonomous Enterprise is embedding intelligence directly into core business processes.
...
✅ The evolution of SAP Business AI and the Autonomous Suite is creating a unified layer where data, processes, and AI agents work together to drive faster and smarter execution.
...
✅ End-to-end automation across Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, and HR is no longer a future concept. It is happening now, and organizations that move early will lead.
...
These conversations strongly validated the direction we are driving at Gainwell Technologies . Building a more intelligent, automated, stable, and business-focused SAP ecosystem.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact

You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. We are looking for individuals who bring enthusiasm, dedication, and fresh perspectives to their work – people who are ready to push boundaries and achieve extraordinary results.
